recalls.Description: Kimpex is recalling its Rush and Arrow II Ski Kits, along with the corresponding adaptor kits, due to a potential safety hazard. These kits, designed to replace skis on snowmobiles, come in various colors including red, green, yellow, white, black, and blue, with the "Kimpex" logo visible on the ski handles. The issue arises from the skis potentially detaching from the snowmobile, which could lead to serious accidents.
recalls.Title: Kimpex Recalls Rush and Arrow II Ski Kits and Adaptor Kits Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Kimpex Inc. at 800-561-6461 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email service@kimpex.com. More details can be found online at https://www.kimpex.com/en-us/page/safety-recalls or by visiting www.kimpex.com and selecting "Safety Recalls." The company is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

Products.Name: Kimpex Rush Ski Kit
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,980 (In addition, 2,600 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Kimpex Rush ski kit and adaptor kit

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the skis falling off snowmobiles. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through Kimpex USA dealers across the country and online at Kimpex.com and various other websites from April 2022 to January 2025, priced between $300 and $500 for the ski kits and approximately $23 for the adaptor kits.

Distributors.Name: Kimpex (U.S.A.) Ltd., of Champlain, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of skis detaching from snowmobiles, leading to potential crashes and serious injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the affected ski kits and adaptor kits immediately and reach out to a Kimpex dealer for a complimentary repair kit.

recalls.metaDescription: Kimpex recalls Rush and Arrow II Ski Kits and adaptors due to a crash hazard. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment is recalling its BD Recon LT avalanche transceivers due to a potential malfunction that may hinder emergency communication. These plastic transceivers feature an LCD screen, a gray body, and a blue top, prominently displaying the Black Diamond logo and model name 'RECON LT.' The affected units were available individually and as part of Black Diamond's Alpine Avy Safety Sets and Recon LT Avy Safety Sets.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Issues Recall for BD Recon LT Avalanche Transceivers Due to Communication Failure Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or via email at 2025reconltrecall@bdel.com. More information is available online at https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ or by visiting www.blackdiamondequipment.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: BD Recon LT Avalanche Transceiver
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,660 (In addition, about 206 were sold in Canada). The transceivers were recalled in 2023 for a different hazard

Images.Caption: Recalled Black Diamond Recon LT avalanche transceiver (front)
Recalled Black Diamond Recon LT avalanche transceiver (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the switch mechanism malfunctioning due to corrosion. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Liberty Mountain, Recreational Equipment Inc. (REI), and various specialty outdoor retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.blackdiamondequipment.com, www.backcountry.com, and www.evo.com from May 2021 through February 2025, priced between $350 and $500.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Corrosion in the switch mechanism may lead to device failure, preventing victim location during an avalanche, posing serious injury or death risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled transceivers and contact Black Diamond for a full refund, replacement, or free repair. No proof of purchase is required, and a pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returns. The repair replaces the original zinc-coated steel insert with a corrosion-resistant stainless-steel one. Consumers can also exchange their BD Recon LT for a BD Guide BT or BD Recon X model, which feature a different switch design, or opt for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls BD Recon LT avalanche transceivers due to communication failure risks. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment is recalling all PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers due to a critical safety issue. These transceivers, made of black plastic with an LCD screen and a yellow mark button, measure approximately 5 inches in length and 3 inches in width. The model name 'PIEPS' is displayed in yellow, while 'PRO IPS' appears in white on the front. The affected serial numbers range from 2326 to 2445 and can be found inside the battery compartment door label.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Recalls PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers Due to Emergency Communication Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or via email at 2024ProIPSrecall@bdel.com. More information is available online at https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ or by visiting www.blackdiamondequipment.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 770 (In addition, about 70 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled PIEPS PRO IPS Avalanche Transceiver - front
Recalled PIEPS PRO IPS Avalanche Transceiver - back
Transceiver's serial number location

Injuries.Name: Black Diamond has received one report of the battery compartment not operating correctly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Alpenglow Sports, Cripple Creek Backcountry, EVO Gear, Liberty Mountain, Mountain Chalet, Mammoth Mountaineering, and other specialty outdoor retailers across the country, as well as online at www.backcountry.com, www.polaris.com, and www.blackdiamondequipment.com from February 2024 through November 2024, priced between $530 and $620.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of loss of power due to insufficient contact force in the battery compartment, potentially leading to failure to locate a victim in an avalanche emergency.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the avalanche transceivers immediately and contact Black Diamond for a full refund or free repair, including shipping. No proof of purchase is required, and a pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returning the recalled units.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers due to emergency communication risks. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds or repairs.

recalls.Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles has issued a recall for the 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles, available in multiple colors. The model is identifiable by an 'M' shaped like a mountain, prominently displayed on the side panels. The vehicle identification number (VIN) features 'R' as its 10th digit, located on the tunnel section or on a decal at the top/rear of the tunnel.
recalls.Title: Recall of 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Textron Specialized Vehicles at 800-279-2281, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also email arcticcatwarranty@textron.com or visit https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/owners/recalls or https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/ and select Recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Artic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobile (black)
Recalled Artic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobile (white)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of cross pins that have become loose or have fallen out. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Textron Specialized Vehicles, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Available at Arctic Cat dealers across the country from September 2023 to June 2024, priced between $14,500 and $16,100.

Hazards.Name: Improperly swagged u-joint pin can lead to loss of steering control, creating a risk of crash.

Remedies.Name: Owners should cease use of the 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobile and reach out to their local authorized Arctic Cat dealer for a complimentary inspection and necessary repairs of the steering column u-joint. If the pin is correctly swagged, no repair is required. If not, the entire steering post assembly will be replaced at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles due to a crash hazard. Contact Textron for inspection and repairs.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment has issued a recall for Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits, identified by Style No. 400073 and date codes 01-2021, 05-2022, 07-2023, 01-2024, and SS1271. The date codes can be found on the rubber heel strap pull-tab and the underside of the toe piece beneath the anti-balling plate. These products, designed for ice climbing, feature aluminum plates with spikes that attach to boots, characterized by their bright green color and black plastic components with webbing straps for ankle fastening. Additionally, the recall encompasses the Neve Strap Binding Accessory Kit (Style No. 400683) and Neve Strap Heel Cup Screw & Nuts (Style No. 400687), sold separately as accessories.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Recalls Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Black Diamond Equipment at 866-306-0865 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email NeveStrapRecall@bdel.com, or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/neve-strap-recall/ or www.blackdiamondequipment.com and select 'Recalls' at the top for further details.

Products.Name: Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 350 (In addition, about 28 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Neve Strap Crampon
Date code located on the underside of the toe piece of the recalled Neve Strap Crampon

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of incidents of the crampons becoming loose or the heel cup becoming detached by hikers. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Black Diamond stores, REI, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at blackdiamond.com, backcountry.com, and REI.com from January 2024 through May 2024, priced around $180.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment, of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to loosening screws that may detach the heel cup from the crampon.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled products immediately and contact Black Diamond Equipment for a full refund. A prepaid return label will be provided for returning the items, after which a refund will be issued. The company is reaching out to all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits due to a fall hazard. Contact for refunds and more info.

recalls.Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles is recalling the Arctic Cat 2024 Catalyst 600 snowmobiles, which include the ZR, Riot, and Mountain models available in various colors. The model names can be found on the side panels, and the VIN will have an 'R' as the 10th character, located on the tunnel section or the decal on the top/rear of the tunnel. This recall is due to a potential crash hazard caused by cracking steering caps that can lead to unstable handlebars.
recalls.Title: Recall of 2024 Arctic Cat Catalyst 600 Snowmobiles Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Textron Specialized Vehicles at 800-279-2281, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be obtained via email at arcticcatwarranty@textron.com or by visiting https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/owners/recalls and selecting 'Recall'.

Products.Name: 2024 Arctic Cat Catalyst 600 Snowmobiles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,670 (In addition, about 891 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Arctic Cat MY24 Catalyst 600 snowmobile

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 95 reports of incidents from dealers during initial snowmobile setups of steering caps cracking while being torqued. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Textron Specialized Vehicles, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Available at Arctic Cat Dealers across the nation from September 2023 to April 2024, priced between $13,800 and $16,100.

Hazards.Name: Cracking steering caps leading to unstable handlebars and crash risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the snowmobiles and contact an authorized Arctic Cat dealer for a free steering cap repair

recalls.metaDescription: Textron recalls Arctic Cat 2024 Catalyst 600 snowmobiles due to a crash hazard from unstable handlebars. Contact for repair details.

recalls.Description: Arctic Cat is recalling all 2017-2021 model year 8000 and 9000 series snowmobiles, along with select 2022 model year 8000 series snowmobiles that have specific VINs ranging from NT808000 to NT808150 and NT100101 to NT101060. These snowmobiles come in various color combinations and feature the "Arctic Cat" branding on the sides and back snow-flap area.
recalls.Title: Recall of Arctic Cat Snowmobiles Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Arctic Cat at 800-279-2281 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at arcticcatwarranty@textron.com or visit https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/owners/recalls. To determine if your snowmobile is affected, locate the vehicle identification number (VIN) on the right side of the snowmobile tunnel near the right footrest.

Images.Caption: Recalled Trail Models: ZR LXR, ZR Sno Pro, ZR LTD, ZR RR, ZR El Tigre, Thundercat
Recalled Crossover Models: XF Cross Country, XF Crosstrek, XF Crosstour, RIOT
Recalled Mountain Models: M King Cat, M SE, M Sno Pro, M Alpha, M Mountain Cat, M Hardcore, XF High Country, XF High Country LTD, and Riot X
Recalled Sport Utility Models: Norseman, and Norseman X
VIN Location: The Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) is stamped into the tunnel near the right-side footrest and on a decal beneath the seat

Injuries.Name: Arctic Cat has received more than 200 reports of clutch failures, including six where clutch fragments hit consumers. Four of those included reports of minor foot and toe injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: Arctic Cat, of Thief River Falls, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available at Arctic Cat dealerships nationwide from September 2016 to January 2024, priced between $8,000 and $22,000.

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration due to potential drive clutch breakage

Remedies.Name: Stop using the snowmobiles and contact Arctic Cat for a free clutch guard installation and inspection. Cracked clutches will be replaced at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: Arctic Cat recalls select snowmobiles due to a laceration hazard. Check if your vehicle is affected and contact for a free remedy.

recalls.Description: Polaris is recalling specific VINs of the 2024 Prostar S4 Titan Adventure 155 ES Snowmobiles due to a potential fire hazard. These snowmobiles are available in black and various custom color options, with the brand name "POLARIS" visible on the front side panel. The vehicle identification number (VIN) and model number can be found on the right side of the frame.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls 2024 Prostar S4 Titan Adventure Snowmobiles Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Visit www.polaris.com and navigate to "Product Safety News" under the "Safety" section, or go to https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to verify if your VIN is affected. You can also reach out to Polaris' owner connections team via email at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online help center at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2024 Prostar S4 Titan Adventure 155 ES Snowmobiles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 240 (In addition, about 290 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 Prostar S4 Titan Adventure 155 ES Snowmobile
Recalled Polaris Snowmobile VIN/Model Number Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of fuel leaking from the recalled snowmobiles. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available at Polaris dealers nationwide from August 2023 through March 2024, priced around $16,100.

Hazards.Name: Fuel leak at the pump flange assembly creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the snowmobiles immediately and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a free repair to replace the pump flange assembly gasket. Polaris has issued a Stop Ride notice and will directly inform registered owners about the remedy.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls 2024 Prostar S4 Titan Adventure snowmobiles due to fire hazards. Check if your vehicle is affected and learn about the remedy.

recalls.Description: Fischer Sports is recalling its RC4 50, RC4 60, and RC4 65 Junior Ski Boots, available in sizes 21.5 to 27.5. The RC4 50 and RC4 60 models were offered in Rhino Grey and Snow colors, while the RC4 65 was available in yellow. Each boot features the 'Fischer' logo on the lower shell, with the model name and size stamped on the upper cuff and sole, respectively.
recalls.Title: Fischer Sports Recalls RC4 Junior Ski Boots Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Fischer Sports US at 800-844-7810, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, email juniorboot.us@fischersports.com, or visit https://www.fischersports.com/voluntary-product-update-junior-alpine-ski-boot-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Fischer RC4 50 Junior Ski Boots
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,460 (In addition, about 114 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Fischer RC4 60 Junior Ski Boots - Snow
Recalled Fischer RC4 60 Junior Ski Boots - Rhino Grey
Recalled Fischer RC4 50 Junior Ski Boots - Rhino Grey
Recalled Fischer RC4 50 Junior Ski Boots - Snow
Recalled Fischer RC4 65 Junior Ski Boots - Yellow
Size location on bottom of ski boot

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an incident of the ski boot cuff rotated. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Fischer Sports US, of Auburn, New Hampshire

Retailers.Name: Available at ski gear retailers nationwide and online through https://www.fischersports.com/us_en/ and other websites from October 2022 to February 2024, priced between $200 and $275.

Importers.Name: Fischer Sports US, of Auburn, Hampshire

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to malfunctioning lock mechanism in ski boot cuff

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ski boots and contact Fischer Sports for a refund, free replacement, or repair instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Fischer Sports recalls RC4 Junior Ski Boots due to a fall hazard. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Bombardier Recreational Products (BRP) has announced a recall for all 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ and Renegade snowmobiles that come with Pilot X skis. These snowmobiles are available in various colors, with the Ski-Doo brand and model name displayed on the side panels. The Pilot X designation is engraved on the upper side of the ski. You can find the vehicle identification number (VIN) on a decal located on the right side of the tunnel beneath the seat, and dealers are available to verify VINs affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: BRP Recalls 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ and Renegade Snowmobiles Over Serious Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BRP at 888-272-9222, available from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, seven days a week. You can also visit https://ski-doo.brp.com/us/en/owner-zone/responsible-rider/safety/safety-recalls/left-pilot-x-ski-may-break-loss-of-control.html, www.ski-doo.com/us/en/owner-zone/maintenance/safety-recalls, or https://ski-doo.brp.com/us/en/ and select 'Safety Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ Snowmobiles with Pilot X Skis
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8,100 (In addition, about 5,600 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled MY 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ Snowmobile
Recalled MY 2024 Ski-Doo Renegade Snowmobile
Pilot X is engraved at the back of the snowmobile’s ski

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 27 reports in the United States of the snowmobile's left ski breaking. There are no reports of injuries in the United States. The firm is aware of one incident in Canada resulting in an injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Bombardier Recreational Products Inc., of Canada

Retailers.Name: Authorized Ski-Doo dealers across the nation, available from June 2023 to January 2024, with prices ranging from $10,000 to $18,000.

Importers.Name: BRP US Inc., of Sturtevant, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of left ski breaking, leading to serious injury and crash potential.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the snowmobiles immediately and contact BRP for a free inspection and repair, which includes replacing the left ski at no charge. BRP will reach out to all affected purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: BRP recalls 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ and Renegade snowmobiles due to a serious injury risk from Pilot X skis. Immediate action recommended.
